pending.body:
finding_summary: The new prompt tells the model to "assign priority using P1 ... P2 ... P3" at `src/contexts/workspace_governance/template_catalog.rs:341`, but the MilestoneBundle contract expects `priority` to be a numeric field (`1=P1, 2=P2, ...`) at `src/contexts/milestone_record/bundle.rs:1144-1146`. That is conflicting guidance for the same field and can drive schema-invalid milestone bundles on backends that do not hard-coerce structured output.
affected_files: ["src/contexts/workspace_governance/template_catalog.rs", "src/contexts/milestone_record/bundle.rs"]
remediation_hint: Reword the prompt to request numeric priority values (`1`, `2`, `3`) while explaining their P1/P2/P3 meaning.

finding_summary: The new `validate_dependency_graph()` helper is not actually safe on malformed bundles. It calls `dependency_graph_analysis()` (`src/contexts/milestone_record/bundle.rs:838-949`), which canonicalizes IDs without running full validation (`src/contexts/milestone_record/bundle.rs:1022-1046`), and `dependency_graph_analysis_from_canonical()` then inserts bead IDs into a `BTreeMap` with silent overwrite semantics (`src/contexts/milestone_record/bundle.rs:714-717`). If two beads normalize to the same ID, the later one wins and the helper can return `Ok(())` or compute the graph against the wrong node instead of reporting the malformed input.
affected_files: ["src/contexts/milestone_record/bundle.rs"]
remediation_hint: Base `validate_dependency_graph()` on `validated_canonical_bundle()` or explicitly reject duplicate normalized bead IDs before building `bead_index`.

status_summary: running: final review restart round 2 -> Plan and Implement
